mnemonics.title: PUE Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: PUE = Total Facility ÷ IT Equipment. Think: 'Put Useful Energy' - lower is better, ideally close to 1.0

acronyms.usage: Measures data center energy efficiency - lower is better
Adjusts CPU power based on workload demands
Measures carbon emissions per unit of data center energy
Centralized desktop management for energy efficiency
Inverse of PUE, expressed as percentage

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle representing total facility energy, then a smaller circle inside for IT equipment energy. PUE is the ratio of outer to inner circle. Closer circles = better efficiency.
Picture one physical server as a large container holding multiple smaller virtual server boxes, each running independently.
A staircase with 4 steps: Active (top) → Idle → Sleep → Hibernation (bottom). Each step down saves more energy but takes longer to 'wake up'.
Energy enters from left, splits: 40-50% goes to IT (useful work), rest goes to cooling, lighting, losses. Aim to maximize the IT portion.

analogies.analogy: PUE is like fuel efficiency in a car - lower numbers mean less 'wasted' energy, just like higher MPG means less fuel wasted.
Virtualization is like one apartment building (physical server) having many individual apartments (virtual machines) instead of many separate houses.
DVFS is like a car's cruise control that automatically slows down when going downhill to save fuel, then speeds up when needed.
Workload consolidation is like carpooling - instead of many cars (servers) each with few passengers (workloads), fewer cars carry more passengers efficiently.
